name: browser_open
description: Open a URL in the browser using browser-use CLI with persistent session support. Use this to navigate to any webpage. For agent-based navigation with AI, use run_agent_kimi instead.

session.description: Session name for persistent browser state

name: browser_state
description: Get current browser state including URL, title, and DOM elements. Use this to inspect page content after navigation. Note: May return empty elements on heavy/JS sites - if so, use browser_html instead.

session.description: Session name to query state for

name: browser_screenshot
description: Take a screenshot of the current page. Always works as a debugging primitive - best tool when state returns empty elements or page has complex JS.

session.description: Session name to take screenshot from

name: browser_html
description: Get HTML content from the current page. Works even when browser_state returns empty elements. Essential for link discovery and parsing page content.

session.description: Session name to get HTML from

name: browser_eval
description: Execute lightweight JavaScript in the browser context for DOM queries. Use when browser_state is empty but you need specific page data like location.href or document.title. For more complex automation, use run_agent_kimi instead.

session.description: Session name to execute JS in

name: run_agent_kimi
description: Run browser-use Agent with Kimi/Moonshot OpenAI-compatible LLM. Use when you need AI-driven browser automation with custom LLM parameters. Required for complex multi-step flows that need reasoning. Use browser_open/browser_screenshot for simple navigation and debugging.

task.description: Task description for the agent to execute

temperature.description: Temperature setting - must be 1 for kimi-k2.5

name: crop_qr_candidates
description: Crop potential QR code regions from a screenshot. Use this to extract candidate QR codes from login/demo pages before attempting to decode. For extracting QR from HTML data:image, use extract_data_images instead.

output_dir.description: Directory to save cropped candidate images

name: extract_data_images
description: Extract base64-encoded images (especially QR codes) from HTML page content. Use this when the page embeds QR codes as data:image/png;base64 in the HTML. For extracting QR from screenshots, use crop_qr_candidates instead.

html_json_path.description: Path to HTML JSON file (from browser_html output)
